page: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation, 4 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. Local storage is bundled with 160 GB of SSD capacity, while no dedicated GPU is present.

In benchmark evaluations, the cpx32 demonstrates strong single-core performance, scoring 1975.0 in Geekbench single-core tests and 3040.68 in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, placing it in the top 25%. Large block memory write bandwidth is also strong at 105.43 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth fall into the average category, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6058.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 12106.51. The primary performance bottleneck is observed in static web serving throughput, which is weak at 4.05 GB/sec, and medium-model LLM prompt processing, which registers at a lower tier of 22.0 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this server as a budget-friendly option for compute-focused workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight database operations, as evidenced by its average Passmark database score of 4225.08 and Redis throughput of 10.32 million operations per second. It is less suitable for high-throughput web serving or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the QEMU hypervisor, it supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and AES. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write speeds, though multi-core metrics and memory read bandwidth remain average. A notable limitation is its weak static web serving throughput of 4.05 GB/sec. Given its shared CPU model and 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the cpx32 offers a cost-effective profile for budget-conscious compute tasks. It is best suited for software compilation, lightweight database operations, and single-threaded processing workloads rather than high-throughput web hosting or intensive AI inference.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for budget-friendly compute workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage for budget compute tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for single-threaded and compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
160 GB of local SSD storage included
Strong single-core CPU and memory write performance
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AES
Best suited for software compilation and lightweight database tasks
